rulings.Case Title: WOOD ROBBINS, LLP VS. HILDA LAI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Order Confirming Arbitration Award And For Judgment In Conformance Therewith
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 14, 2018, Line 1, PETITIONER WOOD ROBBINS, LLP Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Order Confirming Arbitration Award And For Judgment In Conformance Therewith Petitioner Wood Robbins, LLP's petition for order confirming arbitration award is granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order and proposed judgment submitted with the moving papers. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ABRAHAM RASCON VS. CSAA INSURANCE GROUP
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion On Petition And Motion On Petition To Compel Binding Arbitration And Request For Declaratory Relief
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 14, 2018, Line 3, PETITIONER ABRAHAM RASCON'S Motion On Petition And Motion On Petition To Compel Binding Arbitration And Request For Declaratory Relief Claimant Abraham Rascon's petition to compel arbitration is denied without prejudice to being refiled in Contra Costa County Superior Court. This action is transferred to Contra Costa County Superior Court to allow that court to make a determination regarding the gateway issue whether Mr. Rascon is an insured under CSAA's policy. Mr. Rascon shall pay all required transfer fees. There is no basis for San Francisco venue and the evidence submitted by respondent CSAA Insurance Group establishes that the case should be adjudicated in Contra Costa County. (Marcus Dec., par. 2; Exs. 1 and 2.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for CSAA is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and judgment and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DALE DUNCAN ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Charging Orders, Assignment Order, Restraining Order, And For Appointment Of Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 14, 2018, Line 1: PLAINTIFF DALE DUNCAN, MARTA MUNOZ MENDOZA Notice Of Motion For Charging Orders, Assignment Order, Restraining Order, And For Appointment Of Receiver. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KARA L. ESBORG VS. COMCAST CORP. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 14, 2018, Line 3: DEFENDANT PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY'S Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is denied in part and granted in part as follows. Plaintiff belatedly provided verification to her responses to Requests for Production. Requests 11-14 were overly broad, but Plaintiff's responses were not unequivocal. She stated that she had produced numerous documents, but she did not state that such documents represented the universe of such documents in her custody and control. The parties are ordered to meet and confer within 10 days and to attempt to agree on more narrowly tailored requests for production. Plaintiff shall within 15 days serve verified responses to those requests. Such responses may state, if true, that all responsive documents have already been served. If not all responsive documents have not previously been produced, they shall be within 15 days. Plaintiff may also assert any proper objections. This order is without prejudice to Pacific Bell Telephone bringing a further motion to compel for Requests 11-14 as modified. No sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LUIS ANGEL GANTE VS. HUI WU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order On The Stipulation For Filing A Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 14, 2018, Line 18, PLAINTIFF LUIS GANTE'S Motion For Order On The Stipulation For Filing A Second Amended Complaint Plaintiff Luis Gante's motion to file a second amended complaint is denied. While California has a liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings, that policy does not apply to this motion. Mr. Gante has known about the facts supporting his request for punitive damages since before this case was filed and apparently has desired to make such a request since the outset of this case. Yet he twice moved for and received a continuance of the trial without moving to amend his complaint to seek punitive damages and now seeks to do so less than two weeks before trial. Granting this motion would be prejudicial to defendant Hui Wu since there is insufficient time before the trial to take discovery and adjudicate motions pertaining to the request for punitive damages. The court expresses no view whether the allegation that an alleged violation of Vehicle Code 23123(a) is sufficient to allege a cognizable request for punitive damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and judgment and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES AUSTIN ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Hearing On Defendants Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 14, 2018, Line 8, Amended Notice Of Hearing On Defendants Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Defendants Anne Kihagi, Renka Prop, LLC, and Julia Mwangi's motion for judgment on the pleadings on both causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiffs James Austin and Raymond Schreiber is denied as to both causes of action. The motion is an impermissible motion for reconsideration of the March 12, 2018 order denying defendants' first motion for judgment on the pleadings. The request to take judicial notice of Ms. Mwangi's declarations is denied since the content of those declarations is subject to dispute by the parties. Plaintiffs allege sufficient ultimate facts to support both of their causes of action. The complaint alleges that plaintiffs discovered that Ms. Mwangi had left the apartment December 2016, which is sufficient to defeat this motion to the extent that it is based on the one-year statute of limitations. While the request to take judicial notice of Mr. Austin's testimony in CCSF v. Kihagi is granted, that testimony does not establish that either of the plaintiffs' claims lack merit as a matter of law. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KEYHAN MOHANNA ET AL VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Duke Partners Ii Llc'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint (Tentative Rulings Only Part 1 Of 2)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 14, 2018, Line 12, Defendant Duke Partners Ii Llc'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint (Tentative Rulings Only Part 2 Of 2) The following further guidance is provided to the plaintiffs: 1) claims based on a contract term including a good faith covenant must identify the contract or contracts containing the term and the parties to the contract and allege how the contract term was breached; 2) claims alleging fraud must be alleged with particularity; 3) each claim must show that the plaintiff or plaintiffs alleging the claim has standing to do so; 4) claims based on a legal duty must allege the scope of the duty and how the duty was breached; 5) any allegation that the accrual date for a claim is delayed or the statute of limitations is suspended for some period of time must be supported by an allegation stating the reason for the delayed accrual or suspension of the limitations period and the ultimate facts supporting the delayed accrual or suspension of the limitations period; 6) any claim relating to the ownership or possession of real property must allege the date that the claim accrued and who owned or possessed the real property on that date to the best of the information currently available to plaintiffs; and 7) a quiet title claim must be verified per CCP section 761.020. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Duke is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and judgment and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.(end of part 2 of 2) =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KEYHAN MOHANNA ET AL VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Duke Partners Ii Llc'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 14, 2018, Line 12, Defendant Duke Partners II Llc'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint (part 1 of 2) Defendant Duke Partner's II LLC's demurrer to all causes of action alleged against it in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiffs Keyhan Mohanna and 3H Renovation Services, is sustained with 60 days leave to amend as to all causes of action alleged against Wells Fargo. The demurrer is sustained on the grounds that the first amended complaint as a whole and all causes of action alleged therein are uncertain. The first amended complaint, like the initial complaint, is largely unintelligible, contains many irrelevant and/or vague and ambiguous allegations, alleges many repetitive conclusions without providing ultimate facts, and violates the requirement of CCP section 425.10 that "A complaint ¿ shall contain ¿ [a[ statement of the facts constituting the cause of action, in ordinary and concise language." Plaintiffs are given one final opportunity to allege one or more viable claims supported by sufficient allegations of ultimate facts. The second amended complaint should not exceed thirty pages and should avoid repetition wherever possible. For each cause of action alleged therein, the second amended complaint must identify which plaintiff or plaintiffs is bringing that cause of action against which defendant or defendants. Because there are not insignificant issues whether any of the claims plaintiffs may allege are time-barred, the second amended complaint must allege the dates that all events supporting liability occurred to the best of the information currently available to plaintiffs. The second amended complaint must also allege the name of the person or persons who committed all acts and omissions on which plaintiffs seek to impose liability on any entity defendant to the best of the information currently available to plaintiffs. To the extent that any cause of action pertains to specific premises located at 1405 Greenwich Street, the cause of action must also allege which unit or units is the subject of the cause of action. Plaintiffs are admonished to review defendants' demurrers carefully and to address, if they are able to do so, all asserted deficiencies in the first amended complaint identified in those demurrers. In the event that defendants file demurrers or other motions challenging the sufficiency of the second amended complaint, plaintiffs' opposition memoranda need not repeat what plaintiffs alleged in the second amended complaint and must not exceed fifteen 28-line pages in 12 point or greater font absent permission of the court (end of part 1 of 2)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'S Demurrer The First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Defendant Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'S Demurrer The First Amended Complaint (part 1 of 2) Defendant Well Fargo Bank, N.A.'s demurrer to all causes of action alleged against it in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiffs Keyhan Mohanna and 3H Renovation Services, is sustained with 60 days leave to amend as to all causes of action alleged against Wells Fargo. The demurrer is sustained on the grounds that the first amended complaint as a whole and all causes of action alleged therein are uncertain. The first amended complaint, like the initial complaint, is largely unintelligible, contains many irrelevant and/or vague and ambiguous allegations, alleges many repetitive conclusions without providing ultimate facts, and violates the requirement of CCP section 425.10 that "A complaint ¿ shall contain ¿ [a[ statement of the facts constituting the cause of action, in ordinary and concise language." Plaintiffs are given one final opportunity to allege one or more viable claims supported by sufficient allegations of ultimate facts. The second amended complaint should not exceed thirty pages and should avoid repetition wherever possible. For each cause of action alleged therein, the second amended complaint must identify which plaintiff or plaintiffs is bringing that cause of action against which defendant or defendants. Because there are not insignificant issues whether any of the claims plaintiffs may allege are time-barred, the second amended complaint must allege the dates that all events supporting liability occurred to the best of the information currently available to plaintiffs. The second amended complaint must also allege the name of the person or persons who committed all acts and omissions on which plaintiffs seek to impose liability on any entity defendant to the best of the information currently available to plaintiffs. To the extent that any cause of action pertains to specific premises located at 1405 Greenwich Street, the cause of action must also allege which unit or units is the subject of the cause of action. Plaintiffs are admonished to review defendants' demurrers carefully and to address, if they are able to do so, all asserted deficiencies in the first amended complaint identified in those demurrers. In the event that defendants file demurrers or other motions challenging the sufficiency of the second amended complaint, plaintiffs' opposition memoranda need not repeat what plaintiffs alleged in the second amended complaint and must not exceed fifteen 28-line pages in 12 point or greater font absent permission of the court. The following further guidance is provided to the plaintiffs: 1) claims based on a contract term including a good faith covenant must identify the contract or contracts containing the term and the parties to the contract and allege how the contract term was breached; 2) claims alleging fraud must be alleged with particularity; 3) each claim must show that the plaintiff or plaintiffs alleging the claim has standing to do so; 4) claims based on a legal duty must allege the scope of the duty and how the duty was breached; 5) any allegation that the accrual date for a claim is delayed or the statute of limitations is suspended for some period of time must be supported by an allegation stating the reason for the delayed accrual or suspension of the limitations period and the ultimate facts supporting the delayed accrual or suspension of the limitations period; 6) any claim relating to the ownership or possession of real property must allege the date that the claim accrued and who owned or possessed the real property on that date to the best of the information currently available to plaintiffs; and 7) a quiet title claim must be verified per CCP section 761.020. (end of part 1) =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: YUSON WEI YUE LUI VS. ANNA YIT NOR LUI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deft Ng Sau Fong Lui'S Responses To Interrogatories And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 14, 2018, Line 6: PLAINTIFFS YUSON LUI, SUSAN LUI, and DICK LIU'S And Motion To Compel Defendant Ng Sau Fong Lui's Responses To Interrogatories And Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted. Defendant shall serve code compliant responses within 15 days. Where applicable, the responses should interpret the term "incident" as it is defined in the interrogatories. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $630 in attorney's fees plus $69.95 in costs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KAREN Y UCHIYAMA VS. GLORIA LOPEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 14, 2018, Line 15, CROSS DEFENDANT KAREN UCHIYAMA MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND Cross-defendant Karen Uchiyama's motion to set aside the default entered against her on September 10, 2018 is granted. Ms. Uchiyama has made an ample showing of excusable neglect, surprise, mistake, and inadvertence warranting the setting aside of the default per CCP section 473(b). The default should never have been requested and, once entered, cross-complainant Gloria Lopez should have stipulated to it being vacated. Because Ms. Uchiyama had served a demurrer to the cross-complaint notwithstanding that it had been rejected for filing and Mr. Uchiyama had also served a notice of unavailability, counsel for cross-complainant Gloria Lopez had no reason to believe that Ms. Uchiyama was unable or unwilling to defend against the cross-complaint. Under these circumstances, the conduct of Ms. Lopez's counsel in seeking entry of default and refusing to stipulate to have it set aside falls below the standards of civility and professionalism expected of all counsel appearing in California courts. Ms. Uchiyama has until November 28, 2018 to file her demurrer to the cross-complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Ms. Uchiyama is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and judgment and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
